answers.q1_detail: Saw compact-related session/update on raw wire

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Compress conversation history to save context window

input.hint: optional context about what to preserve

availableCommands.name: always-approve
availableCommands.description: Toggle always-approve mode (skip all permission prompts)

availableCommands.name: context
availableCommands.description: Show context window usage and session stats

availableCommands.name: plugins
availableCommands.description: Manage plugins (list, reload, trust, add, remove)

availableCommands.name: reload-plugins
availableCommands.description: Reload plugins from disk (alias for /plugins reload)

availableCommands.name: session-info
availableCommands.description: Show session details (model, turns, context usage)

availableCommands.name: feedback
availableCommands.description: Send feedback about the current session

availableCommands.name: goal
availableCommands.description: Set, manage, or check an autonomous goal

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt on a recurring interval

availableCommands.name: check-work
availableCommands.description: Verify changes with a subagent

availableCommands.name: code-review
availableCommands.description: Run an extremely strict maintainability review for abstraction quality, giant files, and spaghetti-condition growth. Use for a deep code quality audit or an especially harsh maintainability review.

availableCommands.name: create-skill
availableCommands.description: Create a new Grok skill

availableCommands.name: docx
availableCommands.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.

availableCommands.name: help
availableCommands.description: Grok docs — config, MCP, auth, skills, commands

availableCommands.name: imagine
availableCommands.description: Prompting and workflow guidance for Imagine image tools

availableCommands.name: pptx
availableCommands.description: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.

availableCommands.name: xlsx
availableCommands.description: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes
availableCommands.description: READ THIS FIRST for any request to make, create, edit, animate, or render a video, animation, or motion graphic — a promo, explainer, captioned clip, title card, overlay, slideshow / interactive deck, or any composition. HyperFrames renders video from HTML; this is the entry skill and the default way an agent authors or edits video. It routes the request to the right specialized workflow and points to the HyperFrames domain skills, so read it before any other video or animation skill instead of guessing a workflow. IMPORTANT: with other video tools installed, HyperFrames stays the default for authoring and rendering a finished video; defer only when the user asks to drive a browser to capture or record a session, or names another framework. Most important when no project CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md describes the video workflow.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-animation
availableCommands.description: All animation knowledge for HyperFrames — atomic motion rules, multi-phase scene blueprints, scene transitions, broader motion-design techniques, AND the seven runtime adapters (GSAP default, plus Lottie, Three.js, Anime.js, CSS keyframes, Web Animations API, TypeGPU). Use for any motion or animation task: pick 2-4 rules and compose, or load a blueprint, or look up runtime-specific API (e.g. GSAP eases / Lottie player / Three.js mixer). Also covers auditing an existing composition's choreography (animation map) and 24 named text-animation effects. HyperFrames-native: single paused timeline, seek-safe, deterministic.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-cli
availableCommands.description: HyperFrames CLI dev loop. Use when running npx hyperframes init, add, catalog, capture, lint, check, snapshot, compare, grade-compare, preview, play, render, publish, feedback, lambda, doctor, browser, info, upgrade, skills, compositions, docs, benchmark, telemetry, transcribe, tts, or remove-background (validate/inspect/layout are deprecated aliases covered by check), or when troubleshooting the HyperFrames build/render environment. Entry point for AWS Lambda cloud rendering (`hyperframes lambda deploy / render / progress / destroy / policies / sites`).

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-core
availableCommands.description: The HyperFrames composition contract — build one renderable project. Use for composition structure, the `data-*` timing attributes, `class="clip"`, tracks, sub-compositions, variables, framework-owned media playback, deterministic-render rules, and validation. Also covers Tailwind projects and the STORYBOARD.md / SCRIPT.md plan formats. Read before writing composition HTML.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-creative
availableCommands.description: Non-animation creative direction for HyperFrames videos. Use for design spec (frame.md / design.md) handling, palettes, typography, narration, beat planning, audio-reactive visuals, composition patterns, and brand / style decisions. For atomic motion patterns and scene blueprints, use `hyperframes-animation`.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-keyframes
availableCommands.description: Use when a HyperFrames composition needs seek-safe 2D/3D keyframes, GSAP timelines, CSS keyframes, Anime.js, WAAPI, FLIP, paths, masks, SVG morph/draw, text trails, 3D depth, or `hyperframes keyframes` diagnostics. Don't use for broad scene strategy, brand design, media sourcing, captions, or general video planning.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-registry
availableCommands.description: Install and wire registry blocks and components into HyperFrames compositions. Use when running hyperframes add, installing a block or component, wiring an installed item into index.html, or working with hyperframes.json. Covers the add command, install locations, block sub-composition wiring, component snippet merging, registry discovery, and authoring a new block or component to contribute upstream (idea → scaffold → validate → PR).

availableCommands.name: media-use
availableCommands.description: Agent Media OS, the single skill for every media need in a HyperFrames project. Resolve BGM, SFX, image, icon, brand logo, voice, color grade, or LUT into a frozen local file or paste-ready block + ledger record (one verb, `resolve`); generate via TTS / music / image models when the catalog misses; produce voiceover, transcription, captions, and background removal through one shared audio engine; operate on media (cut / reframe / transform); and reuse assets across projects. Keeps search noise on disk, hands the agent one path or block. Use for any audio, image, icon, logo, voiceover, caption, color-grading, or media-asset need.

availableCommands.name: product-launch-video
availableCommands.description: Turn a product or marketing URL, pasted script, or brief into a product launch / promo video — SaaS promos, feature reveals, product demos, app and company launches. Use when the user wants to market, launch, promote, or reveal a product; the default for any commercial URL. Not a general site tour (/website-to-video). Unclear → /hyperframes.

availableCommands.name: agy-nano-banana-batch
availableCommands.description: 用 Google Nano Banana 系列模型批次產圖／改圖：路線A＝Antigravity CLI（agy，訂閱 OAuth headless）、 路線B＝Vertex AI 指名模型（NB2 Lite 量產／NB Pro 精品）；兩路線細節、選路規則、參數全在本文。 當使用者要批次/量產產圖、改圖、圖生圖(i2i)、換天/重打光/換臉/老照片修復等玩法量產、 或用訂閱額度/GCP 抵免額產圖時使用。觸發詞：agy、Antigravity、Nano Banana、NB2、NB2 Lite、NB Pro、 批次產圖、量產圖、圖生圖、i2i、Vertex 產圖、gemini image、gemini-3-pro-image、gemini-3.1-flash-lite-image、plays.json、ImagePaths。

availableCommands.name: chatgpt-web-batch-image
availableCommands.description: Use when the user explicitly requests Playwright automation of the ChatGPT website to parse a supported Markdown prompt list and attempt sequential image generation and download; do not use for API generation, exact model or image controls, parallel generation, or guaranteed outputs.

availableCommands.name: cross-model-dispatch
availableCommands.description: Invoke another installed model through the shared headless dispatcher when the user names a model, requests a second opinion, or the current executor lacks a needed modality or capability. Do not dispatch routinely when the current model can complete and verify the task directly.

availableCommands.name: de-ai-copy-script
availableCommands.description: 文案腳本去除AI味：去除繁中行銷文案、產品說明、影片旁白／SCRIPT 中的 AI 寫作痕跡， 改得更自然、具體、少機器腔。觸發詞：去AI腔、去AI味、人性化文案、humanizer、 去掉機器感、文案改自然、旁白去AI、/de-ai-copy-script。 不要用於純程式、API／workflow 規格、n8n 節點說明、commit／PR 技術描述， 除非使用者明確要求「只刪廢話」或指定規格模式。

availableCommands.name: design-reference-router
availableCommands.description: Route design work that needs real visual references, reusable UI or 3D assets, non-medical human-body references, or OpenArt image/video generation. Use for HyperFrames style frames, web/UI component inspiration, WebGL/glTF assets, 2D-to-3D selection, skeleton/mesh references, and small visual shortlists before implementation; do not use for medical reconstruction or generic media retrieval already owned by media-use.

availableCommands.name: n8n-workflow-helper
availableCommands.description: n8n workflow 操作輔助（CLI 腳本）：修改前備份、LINE 訊息格式、errorWorkflow 串接、Google Sheets 讀寫、結構驗證。自動觸發於任何涉及 n8n workflow 的任務。

availableCommands.name: playwright-cli
availableCommands.description: Automate browser interactions, test web pages and work with Playwright tests.

availableCommands.name: requesting-code-review
availableCommands.description: Request or handle a material code review when the user asks, a shared/public/high-risk change reaches a convergence point, or review feedback must be evaluated. Routine low-risk edits can rely on direct tests and read-back.

availableCommands.name: subagent-driven-development
availableCommands.description: Execute a prepared multi-step plan with subagents when parallelism, specialization, or context isolation materially helps. Use risk-based checkpoints instead of mandatory per-task review or a fixed serial loop.

availableCommands.name: sync-shared
availableCommands.description: 共享同步中心的「動腦判斷」入口。當你要把 .agent-shared 共享核心發佈到 Claude Code、Codex、Grok、AntiGravity/Gemini、Claude Desktop、ZCode，或檢查/處理漂移、回收本地修改時使用。它負責判斷，實際寫入交給 sync-all.ps1、sync-mcp.ps1 與 ensure-skill-links.ps1。

availableCommands.name: systematic-debugging
availableCommands.description: 遇到 bug／測試失敗／行為異常時，先系統性找出根因再動手，不亂猜亂補。 當你要修任何技術問題（workflow 沒跑、hook 沒觸發、頁面沒渲染、編碼失敗、 改一次壞一次、已試過多次還是不對）時使用。 觸發詞：debug、除錯、找不到原因、修不好、越改越壞、測試失敗、hook 沒生效、 root cause、根因、卡住了、為什麼會這樣。

availableCommands.name: using-superpowers
availableCommands.description: Diagnose skill routing when the user asks which skill to use, no direct catalog match exists, or skill discovery appears broken. Do not run this router for ordinary tasks that already match a domain or host-native skill.

availableCommands.name: web-design-shots
availableCommands.description: 前端視覺設計審閱用的多斷點截圖工具(Node Playwright)。當你在跟使用者迭代前端視覺/排版/RWD、需要對本地 dev server 或 staging 截圖看畫面、或要做改版前後的視覺迴歸比對時使用。觸發詞:前端設計、視覺、排版、RWD、響應式、截圖、screenshot、design review、改版對比、視覺迴歸。

availableCommands.name: wp-lovenfit
availableCommands.description: lovenfit.com WordPress 操作輔助：頁面修改前備份、live page 保護、SEO 稽核、WPCode 規則、手機優先驗證、品牌一致性。自動觸發於任何涉及 WordPress 或 lovenfit 的任務。

availableCommands.name: build-with-ai
availableCommands.description: Build AI apps on SpaceXAI (XAI_API_KEY + api.x.ai)

availableCommands.name: design
availableCommands.description: Run the full design-doc-writer and design-doc-reviewer loop until consensus. Produces a polished design document with a PR plan.

availableCommands.name: execute-plan
availableCommands.description: Execute a PR Plan DAG from a design document. Parses the plan, topologically sorts it, implements PRs in parallel using worktree-isolated subagents, runs mandatory orchestrator-level review, and assembles either a Graphite PR stack or a plain-git branch stack depending on tool availability.

availableCommands.name: implement
availableCommands.description: Run the full implement-review-fix loop using implementer and reviewer personas. Supports effort-based multi-reviewer scaling (1-5 reviewers) with automatic specialization selection. Includes memory-based feedback loop that learns from past review patterns. Loops until all reviewers find 0 issues of any severity.

availableCommands.name: pr-babysit
availableCommands.description: Monitor PRs, fix CI failures, address review comments, resolve merge conflicts, and restack stacks. Supports independent PRs, Graphite stacks, and GitHub stacked PRs (gh-stack).

availableCommands.name: resume-claude
availableCommands.description: Continue from a recent Claude Code session

availableCommands.name: resume-codex
availableCommands.description: Continue from a recent Codex session

availableCommands.name: resume-cursor
availableCommands.description: Continue from a recent Cursor session

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: Run a reviewer subagent against uncommitted local changes, a named branch, or a GitHub PR. Local and branch modes write a review file plus a summary to disk. PR mode posts the findings as a PENDING GitHub review for the user to inspect and submit through the UI.

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Compress conversation history to save context window

input.hint: optional context about what to preserve

availableCommands.name: always-approve
availableCommands.description: Toggle always-approve mode (skip all permission prompts)

availableCommands.name: context
availableCommands.description: Show context window usage and session stats

availableCommands.name: plugins
availableCommands.description: Manage plugins (list, reload, trust, add, remove)

availableCommands.name: reload-plugins
availableCommands.description: Reload plugins from disk (alias for /plugins reload)

availableCommands.name: session-info
availableCommands.description: Show session details (model, turns, context usage)

availableCommands.name: feedback
availableCommands.description: Send feedback about the current session

availableCommands.description: Set, manage, or check an autonomous goal

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt on a recurring interval

availableCommands.description: Verify changes with a subagent

availableCommands.description: 共享同步中心的「動腦判斷」入口。當你要把 .agent-shared 共享核心發佈到 Claude Code、Codex、Grok、AntiGravity/Gemini、Claude Desktop、ZCode，或檢查/處理漂移、回收本地修改時使用。它負責判斷，實際寫入交給 sync-all.ps1、sync-mcp.ps1 與 ensure-skill-links.ps1。

availableCommands.name: systematic-debugging
availableCommands.description: 遇到 bug／測試失敗／行為異常時，先系統性找出根因再動手，不亂猜亂補。 當你要修任何技術問題（workflow 沒跑、hook 沒觸發、頁面沒渲染、編碼失敗、 改一次壞一次、已試過多次還是不對）時使用。 觸發詞：debug、除錯、找不到原因、修不好、越改越壞、測試失敗、hook 沒生效、 root cause、根因、卡住了、為什麼會這樣。

availableCommands.name: using-superpowers
availableCommands.description: Diagnose skill routing when the user asks which skill to use, no direct catalog match exists, or skill discovery appears broken. Do not run this router for ordinary tasks that already match a domain or host-native skill.

availableCommands.name: web-design-shots
availableCommands.description: 前端視覺設計審閱用的多斷點截圖工具(Node Playwright)。當你在跟使用者迭代前端視覺/排版/RWD、需要對本地 dev server 或 staging 截圖看畫面、或要做改版前後的視覺迴歸比對時使用。觸發詞:前端設計、視覺、排版、RWD、響應式、截圖、screenshot、design review、改版對比、視覺迴歸。

availableCommands.name: wp-lovenfit
availableCommands.description: lovenfit.com WordPress 操作輔助：頁面修改前備份、live page 保護、SEO 稽核、WPCode 規則、手機優先驗證、品牌一致性。自動觸發於任何涉及 WordPress 或 lovenfit 的任務。

availableCommands.name: build-with-ai
availableCommands.description: Build AI apps on SpaceXAI (XAI_API_KEY + api.x.ai)

availableCommands.name: design
availableCommands.description: Run the full design-doc-writer and design-doc-reviewer loop until consensus. Produces a polished design document with a PR plan.

availableCommands.name: execute-plan
availableCommands.description: Execute a PR Plan DAG from a design document. Parses the plan, topologically sorts it, implements PRs in parallel using worktree-isolated subagents, runs mandatory orchestrator-level review, and assembles either a Graphite PR stack or a plain-git branch stack depending on tool availability.

availableCommands.name: implement
availableCommands.description: Run the full implement-review-fix loop using implementer and reviewer personas. Supports effort-based multi-reviewer scaling (1-5 reviewers) with automatic specialization selection. Includes memory-based feedback loop that learns from past review patterns. Loops until all reviewers find 0 issues of any severity.

availableCommands.name: pr-babysit
availableCommands.description: Monitor PRs, fix CI failures, address review comments, resolve merge conflicts, and restack stacks. Supports independent PRs, Graphite stacks, and GitHub stacked PRs (gh-stack).

availableCommands.name: resume-claude
availableCommands.description: Continue from a recent Claude Code session

availableCommands.name: resume-codex
availableCommands.description: Continue from a recent Codex session

availableCommands.name: resume-cursor
availableCommands.description: Continue from a recent Cursor session

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: Run a reviewer subagent against uncommitted local changes, a named branch, or a GitHub PR. Local and branch modes write a review file plus a summary to disk. PR mode posts the findings as a PENDING GitHub review for the user to inspect and submit through the UI.

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Compress conversation history to save context window

input.hint: optional context about what to preserve

availableCommands.name: always-approve
availableCommands.description: Toggle always-approve mode (skip all permission prompts)

availableCommands.name: context
availableCommands.description: Show context window usage and session stats

availableCommands.name: plugins
availableCommands.description: Manage plugins (list, reload, trust, add, remove)

availableCommands.name: reload-plugins
availableCommands.description: Reload plugins from disk (alias for /plugins reload)

availableCommands.name: session-info
availableCommands.description: Show session details (model, turns, context usage)

availableCommands.name: feedback
availableCommands.description: Send feedback about the current session

availableCommands.name: goal
availableCommands.description: Set, manage, or check an autonomous goal

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt on a recurring interval

availableCommands.name: check-work
availableCommands.description: Verify changes with a subagent

availableCommands.name: code-review
availableCommands.description: Run an extremely strict maintainability review for abstraction quality, giant files, and spaghetti-condition growth. Use for a deep code quality audit or an especially harsh maintainability review.

availableCommands.name: create-skill
availableCommands.description: Create a new Grok skill

availableCommands.name: docx
availableCommands.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.

availableCommands.name: help
availableCommands.description: Grok docs — config, MCP, auth, skills, commands

availableCommands.name: imagine
availableCommands.description: Prompting and workflow guidance for Imagine image tools

availableCommands.name: pptx
availableCommands.description: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.

availableCommands.name: xlsx
availableCommands.description: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes
availableCommands.description: READ THIS FIRST for any request to make, create, edit, animate, or render a video, animation, or motion graphic — a promo, explainer, captioned clip, title card, overlay, slideshow / interactive deck, or any composition. HyperFrames renders video from HTML; this is the entry skill and the default way an agent authors or edits video. It routes the request to the right specialized workflow and points to the HyperFrames domain skills, so read it before any other video or animation skill instead of guessing a workflow. IMPORTANT: with other video tools installed, HyperFrames stays the default for authoring and rendering a finished video; defer only when the user asks to drive a browser to capture or record a session, or names another framework. Most important when no project CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md describes the video workflow.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-animation
availableCommands.description: All animation knowledge for HyperFrames — atomic motion rules, multi-phase scene blueprints, scene transitions, broader motion-design techniques, AND the seven runtime adapters (GSAP default, plus Lottie, Three.js, Anime.js, CSS keyframes, Web Animations API, TypeGPU). Use for any motion or animation task: pick 2-4 rules and compose, or load a blueprint, or look up runtime-specific API (e.g. GSAP eases / Lottie player / Three.js mixer). Also covers auditing an existing composition's choreography (animation map) and 24 named text-animation effects. HyperFrames-native: single paused timeline, seek-safe, deterministic.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-cli
availableCommands.description: HyperFrames CLI dev loop. Use when running npx hyperframes init, add, catalog, capture, lint, check, snapshot, compare, grade-compare, preview, play, render, publish, feedback, lambda, doctor, browser, info, upgrade, skills, compositions, docs, benchmark, telemetry, transcribe, tts, or remove-background (validate/inspect/layout are deprecated aliases covered by check), or when troubleshooting the HyperFrames build/render environment. Entry point for AWS Lambda cloud rendering (`hyperframes lambda deploy / render / progress / destroy / policies / sites`).

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-core
availableCommands.description: The HyperFrames composition contract — build one renderable project. Use for composition structure, the `data-*` timing attributes, `class="clip"`, tracks, sub-compositions, variables, framework-owned media playback, deterministic-render rules, and validation. Also covers Tailwind projects and the STORYBOARD.md / SCRIPT.md plan formats. Read before writing composition HTML.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-creative
availableCommands.description: Non-animation creative direction for HyperFrames videos. Use for design spec (frame.md / design.md) handling, palettes, typography, narration, beat planning, audio-reactive visuals, composition patterns, and brand / style decisions. For atomic motion patterns and scene blueprints, use `hyperframes-animation`.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-keyframes
availableCommands.description: Use when a HyperFrames composition needs seek-safe 2D/3D keyframes, GSAP timelines, CSS keyframes, Anime.js, WAAPI, FLIP, paths, masks, SVG morph/draw, text trails, 3D depth, or `hyperframes keyframes` diagnostics. Don't use for broad scene strategy, brand design, media sourcing, captions, or general video planning.

availableCommands.name: hyperframes-registry
availableCommands.description: Install and wire registry blocks and components into HyperFrames compositions. Use when running hyperframes add, installing a block or component, wiring an installed item into index.html, or working with hyperframes.json. Covers the add command, install locations, block sub-composition wiring, component snippet merging, registry discovery, and authoring a new block or component to contribute upstream (idea → scaffold → validate → PR).

availableCommands.name: media-use
availableCommands.description: Agent Media OS, the single skill for every media need in a HyperFrames project. Resolve BGM, SFX, image, icon, brand logo, voice, color grade, or LUT into a frozen local file or paste-ready block + ledger record (one verb, `resolve`); generate via TTS / music / image models when the catalog misses; produce voiceover, transcription, captions, and background removal through one shared audio engine; operate on media (cut / reframe / transform); and reuse assets across projects. Keeps search noise on disk, hands the agent one path or block. Use for any audio, image, icon, logo, voiceover, caption, color-grading, or media-asset need.

availableCommands.name: product-launch-video
availableCommands.description: Turn a product or marketing URL, pasted script, or brief into a product launch / promo video — SaaS promos, feature reveals, product demos, app and company launches. Use when the user wants to market, launch, promote, or reveal a product; the default for any commercial URL. Not a general site tour (/website-to-video). Unclear → /hyperframes.

availableCommands.name: agy-nano-banana-batch
availableCommands.description: 用 Google Nano Banana 系列模型批次產圖／改圖：路線A＝Antigravity CLI（agy，訂閱 OAuth headless）、 路線B＝Vertex AI 指名模型（NB2 Lite 量產／NB Pro 精品）；兩路線細節、選路規則、參數全在本文。 當使用者要批次/量產產圖、改圖、圖生圖(i2i)、換天/重打光/換臉/老照片修復等玩法量產、 或用訂閱額度/GCP 抵免額產圖時使用。觸發詞：agy、Antigravity、Nano Banana、NB2、NB2 Lite、NB Pro、 批次產圖、量產圖、圖生圖、i2i、Vertex 產圖、gemini image、gemini-3-pro-image、gemini-3.1-flash-lite-image、plays.json、ImagePaths。

availableCommands.name: chatgpt-web-batch-image
availableCommands.description: Use when the user explicitly requests Playwright automation of the ChatGPT website to parse a supported Markdown prompt list and attempt sequential image generation and download; do not use for API generation, exact model or image controls, parallel generation, or guaranteed outputs.

availableCommands.name: cross-model-dispatch
availableCommands.description: Invoke another installed model through the shared headless dispatcher when the user names a model, requests a second opinion, or the current executor lacks a needed modality or capability. Do not dispatch routinely when the current model can complete and verify the task directly.

availableCommands.name: de-ai-copy-script
availableCommands.description: 文案腳本去除AI味：去除繁中行銷文案、產品說明、影片旁白／SCRIPT 中的 AI 寫作痕跡， 改得更自然、具體、少機器腔。觸發詞：去AI腔、去AI味、人性化文案、humanizer、 去掉機器感、文案改自然、旁白去AI、/de-ai-copy-script。 不要用於純程式、API／workflow 規格、n8n 節點說明、commit／PR 技術描述， 除非使用者明確要求「只刪廢話」或指定規格模式。

availableCommands.name: design-reference-router
availableCommands.description: Route design work that needs real visual references, reusable UI or 3D assets, non-medical human-body references, or OpenArt image/video generation. Use for HyperFrames style frames, web/UI component inspiration, WebGL/glTF assets, 2D-to-3D selection, skeleton/mesh references, and small visual shortlists before implementation; do not use for medical reconstruction or generic media retrieval already owned by media-use.

availableCommands.name: n8n-workflow-helper
availableCommands.description: n8n workflow 操作輔助（CLI 腳本）：修改前備份、LINE 訊息格式、errorWorkflow 串接、Google Sheets 讀寫、結構驗證。自動觸發於任何涉及 n8n workflow 的任務。

availableCommands.name: playwright-cli
availableCommands.description: Automate browser interactions, test web pages and work with Playwright tests.

availableCommands.name: requesting-code-review
availableCommands.description: Request or handle a material code review when the user asks, a shared/public/high-risk change reaches a convergence point, or review feedback must be evaluated. Routine low-risk edits can rely on direct tests and read-back.

availableCommands.name: subagent-driven-development
availableCommands.description: Execute a prepared multi-step plan with subagents when parallelism, specialization, or context isolation materially helps. Use risk-based checkpoints instead of mandatory per-task review or a fixed serial loop.

availableCommands.name: sync-shared
availableCommands.description: 共享同步中心的「動腦判斷」入口。當你要把 .agent-shared 共享核心發佈到 Claude Code、Codex、Grok、AntiGravity/Gemini、Claude Desktop、ZCode，或檢查/處理漂移、回收本地修改時使用。它負責判斷，實際寫入交給 sync-all.ps1、sync-mcp.ps1 與 ensure-skill-links.ps1。

answers.q4_recommendation: A+C: A primary (raw intercept) because SDK drops non-union types; C still useful if auto-compact sometimes only updates disk
